1462	/-! **D2' onward — handoff (iter-246).** The δ-wrapping half of D2' is now LANDED axiom-clean:
1463	`isIso_sheafifyDelta_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta` reduces the sheafified `δ` on the unit pair to
1464	the η-bridge `IsIso (a_Y.map (η (pullback φ')))` (via `left_unitality_hom` + the W-stable
1465	right-whiskering `W_whiskerRight_of_W` fed by the new converse `W_of_isIso_sheafification`), and
1466	`isIso_pullbackTensorMap_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta` chains it into the reduction brick. So the
1467	SOLE remaining content of D2' is the **η-bridge**
1468	
1469	  `IsIso (a_Y.map (η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')))`.
1470	
1471	This is the commuting square (`sheafifyUnitIso` is its right vertical, built above)
1472	`a_Y.map (η F) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom = (pullbackValIso f 𝒪_X).hom ≫ pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ`.
1473	Transposing across `SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ` (apply `.homEquiv.injective`,
1474	then `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, `homEquiv_unit`,
1475	`leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η`, `homEquiv_counit`) reduces the square to the concrete pushforward-side
1476	identity (sheafification-mate bridge):
1477	
1478	  `sheafAdj.unit.app 𝒪_X ≫ (pushforward φ).map ((pullbackValIso).inv ≫
1479	      a_Y.map (pullback_pre.map ε_pre ≫ presheafAdj.counit) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom)
1480	    = unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ`,
1481	
1482	where `ε_pre = LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom)`. The glue is the leftAdjointUniq
1483	compatibility of `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`/`pullbackIso` (the bridges inside
1484	`pullbackValIso`) — `Adjunction.{homEquiv_,unit_,}leftAdjointUniq_hom_app`,
1485	`leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit` — relating the presheaf and sheaf adjunction units; this is the
1486	unit-side analog of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`, NOT yet assembled (a self-contained next step).
1487	
1488	* **D3'/D4'** (the chart-chase): use `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (L546) over the common trivialising
1489	  cover; on each chart D3' (δ commutes with the open-immersion base-change square — the sole
1490	  genuinely-new mate calculus, analog of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`) localises the sheafified δ,
1491	  the naturality D1' transports to the unit pair, and D2' closes. Each stays inside
1492	  `IsIso (a_Y.map δ …)`, so `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` is the shared entry. -/
1493	
1494	/-- **Codomain identification for the D2' η-bridge.** The sheafification counit identifies the
1495	sheafified presheaf monoidal unit `a_Y.obj 𝟙_` with the sheaf-level structure module
1496	`𝒪_Y = SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf` (`𝟙_ = (unit Y).val` definitionally, and `unit Y` is
1497	already a sheaf, so the counit at it is an isomorphism). This is the right-hand vertical of the
1498	η-bridge square `a_Y.map (η (pullback φ')) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom
1499	= (pullbackValIso f 𝒪_X).hom ≫ pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ` whose commutativity is the remaining
1500	content of D2' (see the handoff note above and `task_results`). Project-local building block. -/
1501	noncomputable def sheafifyUnitIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
1502	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1503	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1504	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
1505	  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
1506	    (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)
1507	
1508	/-- **Presheaf-side mate identity for the η-bridge** (`unit_app_unit_comp_map_η` instantiated).
1509	For a scheme morphism `f : Y ⟶ X` with `φ' = f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom`, the presheaf-of-modules
1510	adjunction unit at the monoidal unit, post-composed with the pushforward of the oplax unit
1511	comparison `η (pullback φ')`, recovers the lax unit `ε (pushforward φ')`. This is the
1512	presheaf-level driver of the D2' η-bridge: under sheafification (via `sheafificationCompPullback`)
1513	it transports to the sheaf identity
1514	`homEquiv (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) = unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ`.
1515	Project-local: it certifies that the project's `presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal` /
1516	`presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal` instances are `Adjunction.IsMonoidal`-compatible, so the Mathlib
1517	mate identity fires for this concrete adjunction. -/
1518	lemma presheafUnit_comp_map_eta {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
1519	    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1520	        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1521	        (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1522	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ').unit.app
1523	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ≫
1524	      (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ').map
1525	        (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ'))
1526	      = Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ') := by
1527	  letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1528	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1529	      (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1530	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ').IsRightAdjoint :=
1531	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ').isRightAdjoint
1532	  exact Adjunction.unit_app_unit_comp_map_η (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ')
1533	
1534	/-- **D2' η-bridge — IsIso reduction to the unit comparison square** (axiom-clean plumbing).
1535	Given the commuting square identifying the sheafified presheaf unit comparison `a_Y.map (η F)`
1536	with the sheaf-level `pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ` through the canonical isos `pullbackValIso` and
1537	`sheafifyUnitIso`, the η-bridge `IsIso (a_Y.map (η (pullback φ')))` follows (the comparison
1538	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ` is an iso since `Opens.map f.base` is always `Final`). This isolates the
1539	SOLE remaining mathematical content of the η-bridge as the square hypothesis `hsq` (= the unit-side
1540	analog of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`, see handoff in `task_results`). Project-local. -/
1541	lemma isIso_sheafifyEta_of_unitSquare {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
1542	    (hsq : letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1543	          (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1544	          (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1545	        (pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).inv ≫
1546	          (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1547	            (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom
1548	          = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) :
1549	    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1550	        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1551	        (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1552	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1553	      (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ'))) := by
1554	  letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1555	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1556	      (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1557	  set a_Y := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
1558	  set F := PresheafOfModules.pullback φ' with hF
1559	  haveI hfin : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
1560	  haveI hpbu : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
1561	    isIso_pbu_of_final f
1562	  have key : a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom
1563	      = (pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
1564	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom :=
1565	    (Iso.inv_comp_eq _).mp hsq
1566	  rw [(Iso.eq_comp_inv sheafifyUnitIso).mpr key]
1567	  exact IsIso.comp_isIso' (IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance hpbu) inferInstance
1568	
1569	/-- **Composite-adjunction `homEquiv` factorisation** (blueprint
1570	`lem:comp_homequiv_factor_sheafify_pullback`, ★ step 3). For composable adjunctions
1571	`adj₁ : L₁ ⊣ R₁` and `adj₂ : L₂ ⊣ R₂`, the hom-set bijection of the composite adjunction

62	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
63	
64	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
65	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
66	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
67	
68	namespace PresheafOfModules
69	
70	open InternalHom Opposite
71	
72	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
73	
74	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
75	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
76	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
77	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
78	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
79	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
80	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
81	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
82	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
83	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
84	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
85	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
86	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
87	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
88	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
89	  exact
90	    { toFun := fun φ =>
91	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
92	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
93	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
94	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
95	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
96	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
97	      invFun := fun r =>
98	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
99	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
100	      left_inv := fun φ => by
101	        ext Y
102	        dsimp only
103	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
104	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
105	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
106	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
107	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
108	        rfl
109	      right_inv := fun r => by
110	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
111	            (restr X.unop
112	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
113	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
114	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
115	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
116	        exact mul_one r }
117	
118	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
119	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
120	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
121	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
122	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
123	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
124	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
125	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
126	    (fun {X Y} f => by
127	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
128	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
129	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
130	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
131	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
132	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
133	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
134	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
135	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
136	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
137	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
138	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
139	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
140	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
141	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
142	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
143	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
144	      rw [hrm] at key
145	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
146	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
147	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
148	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
149	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
150	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
151	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
152	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
153	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
154	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
155	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
156	
157	end PresheafOfModules

159	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
160	
161	namespace Scheme
162	
163	namespace Modules
164	
165	/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/
166	
167	open PresheafOfModules InternalHom Opposite in
168	/-- **Leg (A)∘(B): the sectionwise slice transport of the dual along an open immersion.**
169	
170	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X`, `M : X.Modules`, and an open `V` of `Y` (as `(Opens Y)ᵒᵖ`),
171	this is the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear isomorphism between the two sectionwise values of the Step-4 residual
172	of `dual_restrict_iso`:
173	```
174	  ((pushforward β).obj (dual M.val)).obj V  ≅  (dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V
175	```
176	where `β` is the open-immersion structure ring morphism `Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙
177	X.ringCatSheaf` (`β.app U = (forget₂ _ _).map (f.appIso U).inv`).
178	
179	The construction mirrors `homLocalSection` (the thin-poset `eqToHom`-conjugation slice transport)
180	composed with `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear codomain-unit ring swap of leg
181	(B)): a dual section `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X` over `Over (fV)` is reindexed across
182	`f.opensFunctor` to a dual section over `Over V`, conjugating each component by the structure ring
183	iso `f.appIso`; naturality on the thin poset `Opens Y` is `Subsingleton.elim`. -/
184	noncomputable def sliceDualTransport {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
185	    (M : X.Modules) (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)ᵒᵖ) :
186	    letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
187	      { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
188	    letI β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
189	      Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
190	    (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj (PresheafOfModules.dual M.val)).obj V) ≅
191	      ((PresheafOfModules.dual ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V) := by
192	  -- CONSTRUCTION PLAN (homLocalSection-style leg (A) ∘ restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv leg (B)):
193	  --
194	  -- Write `fV := f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop`.  By `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_obj`,
195	  --   LHS carrier `L = (dual M.val).obj (op fV) = (restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X)`,
196	  --     a `𝒪_X(fV)`-module restricted along `β.app V : 𝒪_Y(V) ⟶ 𝒪_X(fV)` to a `𝒪_Y(V)`-module;
197	  --   RHS carrier `Rr = (restr V.unop ((pushforward β).obj M.val) ⟶ restr V.unop 𝟙_Y)`,
198	  --     a `𝒪_Y(V)`-module via `internalHomObjModule`.
199	  --
200	  -- Build a `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear equivalence `e : L ≃ₗ[𝒪_Y(V)] Rr` and return `e.toModuleIso`.
201	  --
202	  -- `e.toFun φ` (for `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X`) is the dual section over `Over V`
203	  -- whose component at `W : (Over V.unop)ᵒᵖ` (so `W' := W.unop.left ≤ V.unop`, with image
204	  -- `fW' := f.opensFunctor.obj W'`) is
205	  --   `(restr V.unop ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj W  ≃defeq  M.val.obj (op fW')`
206	  --     --[ φ.app (op (Over.mk (f.opensFunctor.map W.unop.hom))) ]-->  X.ring(fW')
207	  --     --[ (f.appIso W').hom : 𝒪_X(fW') ≅ 𝒪_Y(W') ]-->  Y.ring(W')  =  (restr V.unop 𝟙_Y).obj W,

328	noncomputable def dual_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
329	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M : X.Modules) :
330	    (dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f) := by
331	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`.
332	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (dual M) ≪≫ ?_
333	  -- Step 2. Sheafification commutes with pullback.
334	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
335	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) ≪≫ ?_
336	  -- Step 3. Strip the outer sheafification, descending to the presheaf residual.
337	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
338	      (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
339	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL): the presheaf goal
340	  --   `(pullback φ).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((M.restrict f).val)`.
341	  -- H1: replace `pullback φ` with `pushforward β` (β the open-immersion structure ring iso).
342	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
343	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
344	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
345	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
346	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
347	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
348	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
349	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
350	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
351	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
352	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
353	  -- Residual: `(pushforward β).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)`.

459	noncomputable def homLocalSection {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} {ι : Type*}
460	    (U : ι → X.Opens) (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι) (i : ι) :
461	    (CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf).obj (op (U i)) where
462	  app W :=
463	    haveI hle : W.unop.left ≤ U i := W.unop.hom.le
464	    haveI himg : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left) = W.unop.left := by
465	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
466	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr hle
467	    M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg.symm)) ≫
468	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left)) ≫
469	      N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg))
470	  naturality := by
471	    intro A B φ
472	    have hBA : (unop B).left ≤ (unop A).left := ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).le
473	    let κ : (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left ⟶ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left :=
474	      (Opens.map (U i).ι.base).map (homOfLE hBA)
475	    have himgA : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left) = (unop A).left := by
476	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
477	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop A).hom.le
478	    have himgB : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left) = (unop B).left := by
479	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
480	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop B).hom.le
481	    -- naturality of the underlying ab-presheaf morphism of `f i`

483	    -- the two thin-poset square edges agree (`Opens X` is a thin poset)

486	      Subsingleton.elim _ _

489	      Subsingleton.elim _ _

494	          (M.restrict (U i).ι).val.presheaf.map κ.op := by
495	      rw [(M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubM]
496	      exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
497	    -- N-side analogue

499	          N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB))
500	        = N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA)) ≫
501	          N.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op := by
502	      rw [(N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubN]
503	      exact N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
504	    dsimp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.op_map, Functor.op_obj, Functor.comp_obj]

518	    (s : (CategoryTheory.presheafHom F G).obj (op ⊤)) : F ⟶ G :=

547	/-- **A-bridge: compatible local `𝒪_X`-module morphisms glue to a global morphism.**
548	
549	Blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` (~L5592).  Let `X` be a scheme,
550	`M N : X.Modules`, and `{U i}` an indexed open cover of `X`.  If for each `i` we have a
551	morphism `f i : M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι` in `Scheme.Modules (U i)` such
552	that the underlying section maps of `f i` and `f j` agree, *sectionwise*, on every open
553	`V ≤ U i ⊓ U j` (each conjugated into the fixed abelian-group hom-type `M(V) ⟶ N(V)` by the
554	canonical `eqToHom`s from the down-set identity `ι(ι⁻¹V) = V`), then there is a unique global
555	morphism `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules` whose restriction to each `U i` is `f i`.
556	
557	The compatibility hypothesis `hf` is the **sectionwise** overlap-agreement (iter-254 re-sign;
558	this `def` is NOT in `archon-protected.yaml` and has no compiling caller, so the prover owns its
559	signature).  The earlier `HEq` form — comparing the two `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-images of
560	`f i`, `f j` along the two slice-restrictions — was *unsatisfiable*: those images live in
561	sheafifications of pullback presheaves along *different* morphisms, hence in only-isomorphic
562	(not propositionally equal) objects, so no `HEq`-elimination applies and no caller can produce
563	the datum.  The sectionwise form compares only the section maps, which live in the fixed group
564	`M(V) ⟶ N(V)`, and is exactly the data a caller (two local morphisms literally agreeing on
565	overlaps) has at hand.  See blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` sub-step (a).

10	# Coherence of locally trivial line bundles (A.2.c engine)
11	
12	This file is the **iter-256 Lane engine** file-skeleton for the cheap, on-path
13	half of the line-bundle coherence requirement of the A.2.c Quot-scheme
14	embedding (`chap:Picard_QuotScheme`, `chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`).
15	
16	The Quot functor takes coherent quotients of a coherent sheaf; to feed it a line
17	bundle one must know that the line bundle is itself coherent, i.e. a finitely
18	presented `𝒪`-module. The embedding's input is a point of the relative Picard
19	scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k}`, whose carrier is the **locally trivial** predicate
20	`IsLocallyTrivial` of `chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback` (the project-side stand-in
21	for an invertible sheaf). The chapter proves
22	`IsLocallyTrivial M ⟹ M.IsFinitePresentation`, together with a rank-one /
23	flatness record. The general implication "`M` tensor-invertible `⟹` `M` locally
24	free of rank one" (the converse spreading-out, Stacks Lemma 0B8M when stalks are
25	local) is *not* crossed here: the carrier is already locally trivial, so the
26	finite-presentation spreading-out is never needed.
27

121	/-! ## §1. From pointwise triviality to a trivialising cover -/
122	
123	/-- **Trivialising affine cover** (Stacks 01CS / Definition 17.25.1).
124	A locally trivial module `M` admits an indexed trivialising affine cover: an
125	index type `I`, a family of affine opens `U i` covering `X`, and for each `i` a
126	`𝒪_{U_i}`-module isomorphism `M|_{U_i} ≅ 𝒪_{U_i}`.
127	
128	The witness repackages the pointwise existential `IsLocallyTrivial M` (take
129	`I := X` and assign each point its chosen neighbourhood). -/
130	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.exists_trivializing_cover {M : X.Modules}
131	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
132	    ∃ (I : Type u) (U : I → X.Opens),
133	      (∀ i, IsAffineOpen (U i)) ∧ iSup U = ⊤ ∧
134	        ∀ i, Nonempty (M.restrict (U i).ι ≅
135	          SheafOfModules.unit (U i : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) := by
136	  refine ⟨X, fun x => (hM x).choose, fun x => (hM x).choose_spec.2.1, ?_,
137	    fun x => (hM x).choose_spec.2.2⟩
138	  rw [eq_top_iff]
139	  intro x _
140	  rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
141	  exact ⟨x, (hM x).choose_spec.1⟩
142	
143	/-! ## §1b. The canonical finite presentation of the unit module -/
144	
145	section UnitPresentation
146	
147	universe u₁ v₁
148	
149	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
150	  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
151	  [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
152	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
153	
154	/-- The canonical isomorphism `free PUnit ≅ unit R`: the free module on a single
155	generator is the unit module. -/
156	noncomputable def freeUnitIso :
157	    SheafOfModules.free.{u} (R := R) PUnit.{u + 1} ≅ SheafOfModules.unit R :=
158	  Limits.coproductUniqueIso (fun (_ : PUnit.{u + 1}) ↦ SheafOfModules.unit R)
159	
160	/-- The canonical generating sections of `unit R`: one generator (the image of
161	the free generator under `freeUnitIso`). -/
162	noncomputable def unitGenerators : (SheafOfModules.unit R).GeneratingSections where
163	  I := PUnit.{u + 1}
164	  s := (SheafOfModules.unit R).freeHomEquiv freeUnitIso.hom
165	  epi := by
166	    rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
167	    infer_instance
168	
169	/-- The canonical finite presentation of the unit module `unit R`: one generator,
170	no relations (its generating map is an isomorphism, so the relation kernel is the
171	zero object). -/
172	noncomputable def unitPresentation : (SheafOfModules.unit R).Presentation where
173	  generators := unitGenerators
174	  relations :=
175	    { I := PEmpty.{u + 1}
176	      s := PEmpty.elim
177	      epi := by
178	        have hiso : IsIso (unitGenerators (R := R)).π := by
179	          dsimp only [unitGenerators, SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections.π]
180	          rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
181	          infer_instance
182	        have : Mono (unitGenerators (R := R)).π := inferInstance
183	        have hz : Limits.IsZero (Limits.kernel (unitGenerators (R := R)).π) :=
184	          Limits.isZero_kernel_of_mono _
185	        exact ⟨fun g h _ => hz.eq_of_src g h⟩ }
186	
187	instance : (unitPresentation (R := R)).IsFinite where
188	  isFiniteType_generators := ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite PUnit.{u + 1})⟩
189	  isFiniteType_relations := ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite PEmpty.{u + 1})⟩
190	
191	end UnitPresentation
192	
193	/-! ## §2. The trivial presentation on a chart -/
194	
195	/-- **The over↔restrict trivialisation bridge.** Transports the scheme-level
196	trivialisation `e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U` (an isomorphism in
197	`SheafOfModules (↑U).ringCatSheaf`, the category of modules on the *open
198	subscheme* `U`) to a slice-level trivialisation
199	`M.over U ≅ 𝒪_U` (in `SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)`, the category of
200	modules on the *slice site* `J.over U`).
201	
202	The two source categories are equivalent — the open-immersion functor
203	`U.ι.opensFunctor : Opens ↥U ≌ {V ∈ Opens X | V ≤ U} = Over U` induces an
204	equivalence `SheafOfModules (↑U).ringCatSheaf ≌ SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)`
205	identifying `M.restrict U.ι ↦ M.over U` and `unit ↦ unit`. That modules-level
206	lift of `Opens.overEquivalence` is the shared root `Scheme.Modules.overEquivalence`
207	of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SheafOverEquivalence`, and the bridge is the general
208	`Scheme.Modules.chartOverIso` built there as the three-step composite
209	`(restrictOverIso).symm ≪≫ overEquivalence.functor.mapIso e ≪≫ unitOverIso`.
210	
211	This local declaration now **redirects** to that general construction (the
212	iter-258 shared-root pivot), so it carries no `sorry` of its own; the remaining
213	mathematical content (the three isos `overEquivalence` / `restrictOverIso` /
214	`unitOverIso`) lives in the shared-root file where the dual lane consumes it too.
215	Everything else in this engine (`chartPresentation`, `isFinitePresentation`,
216	`isFiniteType`) was already complete modulo this isomorphism. -/
217	noncomputable def chartOverIso (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens)
218	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
219	    M.over U ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) :=
220	  Scheme.Modules.chartOverIso U M e
221	
222	/-- **Finite free presentation on a chart** (Stacks, "Modules of finite
223	presentation"). On a trivialising chart `U` — i.e. with an isomorphism
224	`M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` — the restriction `M.over U` admits the trivial finite free
225	presentation (one generator, no relations), obtained by transporting the
226	canonical presentation of `SheafOfModules.unit` along the trivialisation
227	(through the `chartOverIso` bridge). -/
228	noncomputable def IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens)
229	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
230	    (M.over U).Presentation := by
231	  exact SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
232	    (M := SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) (N := SheafOfModules.over M U)
233	    (chartOverIso M U e).inv (unitPresentation (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U))
234	
235	instance (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens)
236	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
237	    (IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation M U e).IsFinite := by
238	  dsimp only [IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation]
239	  infer_instance
240	
241	/-! ## §3. Finite presentation of a locally trivial line bundle -/
242	
243	/-- **Locally trivial `⟹` finitely presented** (Stacks 0B8M / Lemma 17.25.4).
244	A locally trivial module on a scheme is finitely presented: assemble a
245	`QuasicoherentData` from the trivialising affine cover
246	(`exists_trivializing_cover`), whose presentation at each index is the finite
247	free presentation of `chartPresentation`, and feed it to
248	`SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk`. No stalk computation or
249	neighbourhood-spreading is involved. -/
250	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.isFinitePresentation {M : X.Modules}
251	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
252	    M.IsFinitePresentation := by
253	  obtain ⟨I, U, hUaff, hUtop, hUiso⟩ := hM.exists_trivializing_cover
254	  -- assemble the quasi-coherent data, one finite chart presentation per index;
255	  -- `shrink` lands the index type in the site-object universe demanded by
256	  -- `IsFinitePresentation`'s existential.
257	  let q : M.QuasicoherentData :=
258	    { I := I
259	      X := U
260	      coversTop := by
261	        -- the trivialising affine opens cover the terminal object of the site
262	        intro W x hxW
263	        have hx : x ∈ iSup U := by rw [hUtop]; trivial
264	        rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup] at hx
265	        obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hx
266	        exact ⟨U i ⊓ W, homOfLE inf_le_right, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_left⟩⟩, hi, hxW⟩
267	      presentation := fun i => IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation M (U i) (hUiso i).some }
268	  have hsh : q.shrink.IsFinitePresentation := by
269	    apply SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation.mk
270	    intro i
271	    exact inferInstanceAs (IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation M _ _).IsFinite
272	  exact { exists_quasicoherentData := ⟨q.shrink, hsh⟩ }
273	
274	/-- **Locally trivial `⟹` finite type and quasi-coherent** (corollary of
275	`isFinitePresentation`). Finite presentation implies finite type by Mathlib's
276	`SheafOfModules.instIsFiniteTypeOfIsFinitePresentation` and quasi-coherence by
277	`SheafOfModules.instIsQuasicoherentOfIsFinitePresentation`. -/
278	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.isFiniteType {M : X.Modules}
279	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
280	    M.IsFiniteType := by
281	  haveI := hM.isFinitePresentation
282	  infer_instance
283	
284	/-! ## §4. Rank one and flatness record -/
285	
286	/-- **Chart-local rank one and flatness** (Stacks, "Locally free sheaves":
287	finite locally free of rank `r`). On a trivialising chart the restriction
288	`M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` is free of rank one over `𝒪_U`; in particular flat (`𝒪_U` is a flat
289	module over itself, and flatness is preserved by the isomorphism). The fact is
290	recorded chart-locally — as the rank-one free model `M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U = ⊕_{*} 𝒪_U`
291	the Quot embedding consumes — because Mathlib has no `SheafOfModules`-level
292	locally-free / flat / rank predicate. -/
293	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.chart_free_rank_one {M : X.Modules}
294	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) (x : X) :
295	    ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
296	      Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) := by
297	  exact hM x
298	
299	end LineBundle
300	
301	end Scheme
302	
303	end AlgebraicGeometry
